verifyCommand: uv run pytest && uv run ruff check .
acceptanceCriteria: Verification command passes (uv run pytest && uv run ruff check .)
All non-skipped features completed
All pre-existing tests (test_ingest, test_detectors, test_labels, test_resolve, test_benchmark, test_vision) still pass unchanged
notes: M5: agent + MCP layer for relief-probe — a tool-grounded loan investigator and an MCP server over the same read-only warehouse tools. ENTITY KEY is loan_number (string), NOT npi. Reuse existing modules: warehouse.connect, scoring.composite_ranking, benchmark.labeled_fraud_loans, detectors. DETERMINISTIC-FIRST: the investigate() default path and all tools must be pure-Python and fully testable WITHOUT the `agent` extra (langgraph/langchain-anthropic/mcp). Import any of langgraph/langchain_anthropic/mcp LAZILY inside functions so module import never fails in the core env; tests for LLM/MCP paths use pytest.importorskip. Verify with `uv run pytest && uv run ruff check .` (core env, no extra). Mirror the conventions in the existing codebase (typed, documented, ruff line-length 90). A high score/report is a lead for review, not evidence of fraud — every report carries that disclaimer. Read plans/guardrails.md every iteration. Commit one feature per iteration.

features.title: agent/tools.py — read-only evidence-gathering tools over the warehouse
acceptanceCriteria: New src/relief_probe/agent/__init__.py and src/relief_probe/agent/tools.py (pure Python, no agent-extra imports)

loan_profile(con, loan_number) -> dict of the loan's key fields (borrower_name, naics_code, borrower_state, current_approval_amount, jobs_reported, loan_status, forgiveness_amount, date_approved); returns {} if not found
loan_signals(con, loan_number) -> list of dicts {detector_id, score, evidence} from the signals table (evidence_json parsed)

peer_comparison(con, loan_number) -> dict with amount_per_job, cohort (naics x state), cohort_median_amount_per_job, x_cohort_median, cohort_size; {'available': False} when jobs missing or cohort too small
fraud_case_check(con, loan_number) -> dict {labeled: bool, cases: [...]} from fraud_cases (match_method, match_confidence, source_url)
composite_for(con, loan_number) -> dict {composite_score, n_signals, detectors} or {'flagged': False} if not in signals
gather_evidence(con, loan_number) -> dict bundling all of the above under stable keys
tests/test_agent_tools.py seeds a small warehouse (loans + signals + fraud_cases) and asserts each tool's shape + values, including the not-found / not-flagged / no-cohort branches
Verification passes (uv run pytest && uv run ruff check .)
features.notes: Foundation for everything else. Read-only; never writes. Reuse warehouse.connect. Parse evidence_json with json.loads. Cohort logic can mirror detectors/naics_cohort_outlier (NAICS x state, amount/jobs).

features.title: agent/report.py — InvestigatorReport model + deterministic build_report
acceptanceCriteria: src/relief_probe/agent/report.py with a frozen dataclass InvestigatorReport(loan_number, risk_level, summary, evidence: list[EvidenceItem], alternative_explanations: list[str], recommended_next_steps: list[str], disclaimer: str)
EvidenceItem(claim, source, detail) — source names the tool/query that produced it (grounding)
DISCLAIMER constant: a high score is a statistical lead for review, not evidence of fraud (see RESPONSIBLE_USE.md)

build_report(evidence: dict) -> InvestigatorReport: deterministic, derives risk_level from composite score / n_signals / labeled (e.g. low/elevated/high/critical), writes a factual summary, an evidence row per fired detector + peer comparison + fraud-case match, sensible alternative_explanations (legit high-wage business, EIDL-refinance, data artifacts) and recommended_next_steps
Every evidence row cites a source; build_report uses ONLY the passed evidence (no fabrication)
tests/test_agent_report.py: a flagged+labeled loan -> high/critical with cited evidence; an unflagged loan -> low risk; disclaimer always present
Verification passes
features.notes: Pure Python. Consumes the dict from tools.gather_evidence. Keep risk thresholds simple and documented.

features.title: agent/graph.py investigate() + `relief-probe investigate` CLI (deterministic default)
acceptanceCriteria: src/relief_probe/agent/graph.py with investigate(con, loan_number, *, use_llm=False) -> dict {report: InvestigatorReport, telemetry: {...}}
Default (use_llm=False) path is pure Python: gather_evidence -> build_report; no langgraph/langchain imports at module load

use_llm=True path imports langchain_anthropic/langgraph LAZILY and synthesizes the narrative with claude-opus-4-8 using ONLY tool-fetched facts; raises a clear error if the agent extra or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing
CLI command `relief-probe investigate <loan_number> [--llm/--no-llm]` prints the report (rich) with the disclaimer

tests/test_agent_graph.py: deterministic investigate on a seeded loan returns a populated report + telemetry; LLM path test uses pytest.importorskip('langchain_anthropic') so core-env runs skip it cleanly
Verification passes
features.notes: Deterministic path is the gate. Telemetry can be simple (tool_calls count, path='deterministic'/'llm'). Mirror probity's investigate ergonomics.

features.title: agent/mcp_server.py — expose the tools over MCP + `relief-probe serve-mcp`
acceptanceCriteria: src/relief_probe/agent/mcp_server.py exposing four read-only tools: score_loan, peer_compare, check_fraud_case, investigate (deterministic), each wrapping the agent.tools / graph functions over a warehouse connection
mcp imported LAZILY (module import must not require the agent extra)
CLI command `relief-probe serve-mcp` starts the server (guarded: clear message if `mcp` not installed)
tests/test_mcp_server.py uses pytest.importorskip('mcp') and asserts the tool registry exposes the four tools with the documented names/signatures (no network/stdio needed)
README/NEXT_STEPS updated: Layer 6 (agent + MCP) marked done; CLI table updated
Verification passes
features.notes: Keep the tool surface thin — delegate to agent.tools/graph. The importorskip test keeps the loop green in the core env.
